Title: Pharmaniaga bags RM281.7mil contract from Health Ministry to supply human insulin
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Pharmaniaga Bhd, through wholly-owned subsidiary Pharmaniaga LifeScience Sdn Bhd (PLS), has secured a three-year contract valued at RM281.7 million from the Ministry of Health for the supply of human insulin to healthcare facilities nationwide.
In a statement, the pharmaceutical company said the award reflects the group's established manufacturing capabilities, proven execution track record and continued alignment with national healthcare priorities.
At the same time, it said, the contract strengthens its standing as a key player within the country's pharmaceutical and healthcare ecosystem.
The company said the contract represents 70 per cent of the total tender volume, underscoring Pharmaniaga's scale, operational readiness and capacity to support large-volume, nationwide supply requirements.
Pharmaniaga said it also strengthens the group's recurring revenue base within the public healthcare segment, while further enhancing visibility of earnings over the contract period.
"Importantly, the award reinforces Pharmaniaga's growing role within the biopharmaceutical segment, particularly in the production and supply of critical chronic disease treatments such as insulin.
"As demand for such therapies continues to rise, the group's capabilities in this segment position it to capture long-term growth opportunities, while contributing meaningfully to the development of a more resilient domestic pharmaceutical manufacturing ecosystem," it said.
Pharmaniaga managing director Datuk Zulkifli Jafar said the group is honoured to contribute to the Health Ministry's healthcare priorities, while further strengthening its manufacturing and supply chain capabilities in service of the nation.
"This award reflects the strategic direction we have set under Vision ONE30, which focuses on advancing biopharmaceutical capabilities, reinforcing our generics leadership, enhancing logistics excellence and unlocking value from new growth areas to support long-term, sustainable growth," he said.
He also said that the contract came at a timely juncture, as ongoing geopolitical developments continue to highlight the importance of building a more resilient and self-reliant domestic pharmaceutical ecosystem.
"Strengthening local manufacturing capabilities, particularly for essential and chronic disease treatments such as insulin, will be critical in safeguarding national medicine security and ensuring sustainable access to treatment for the people in the years ahead," Zulkifli added.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Bersatu set to work with PH again? Lies, says Radzi Jidin
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Parti Pribumi Bersatu Malaysia (Bersatu) vice-president, Datuk Dr Radzi Jidin, has denied claims that a return to cooperation with Pakatan Harapan (PH) is on the cards.
In a Facebook posting, he labelled the claims as "malicious lies" spread by certain quarters.
"There is a malicious lie going around claiming that Bersatu will work again with PH.
"Even while we are busy with Aidiladha, they are busying themselves with lies.
"They may be masked, but I know who is behind the lie," he said.
Radzi, however, neither elaborated on who he was referring to nor explained the motive behind the allegation.
Previous reports had hinted at Bersatu's return to cooperation with PH, following the former's ongoing tensions with Pas.
Last week, Pas president Tan Sri Abdul Hadi Awang
took aim at Bersatu,
saying the Islamist party is ready to review its ties and could very well move forward without its coalition partner in the next general election if their cooperation threatens electoral results.
Bersatu, in response, told
Pas to stop meddling
in its internal affairs and respect disciplinary decisions made to stabilise the party.
Meanwhile, Bersatu information chief, Datuk Tun Faisal Ismail Aziz, also refuted the claim, saying that it was never discussed in the party's meetings at any platform.
Tun Faisal said such a cooperation "did not make sense" as Bersatu was among the parties responsible for the PH government's downfall following the 14th General Election.
DAP secretary-general Anthony Loke had previously
cautioned PH to be wary
when cooperating with any party, specifically Bersatu and Pas.
Loke had said that this was because PH had a bitter experience in working with the two parties.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: IOI Properties Q3 net profit jumps more than threefold to RM258.11mil
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: IOI Properties Group Bhd's net profit jumped 3.39 times year-on-year to RM258.11 million in the third quarter ended March 31, 2026, for the current financial year (Q3 FY26) from RM76.13 million in the same period last year.
Revenue also surged to RM1.05 billion from RM755.16 million previously.
In a filing with Bursa Malaysia today, the property developer said the increase in both revenue and earnings was mainly due to the consolidation of Singapore-based Scottsdale Properties Pte Ltd following the acquisition of the remaining 50.1 per cent equity interest last year.
It said the stellar performance was also due to higher contributions from its property development segment, following the recognition of RM130 million from the sale of land in Melaka in the current quarter, as well as stronger contributions from the property investment segment, driven by higher occupancy at IOI Central Boulevard Tower.
For the nine-month period ended March 31, 2026, IOI Properties said its net profit soared to RM1.63 billion from RM240.08 million previously, while revenue surged to RM3.06 billion from RM2.17 billion previously.
Moving forward, group chief executive officer Datuk Lee Yeow Seng said the group's property investment segment is poised to continue its upward trajectory, supported by the improving physical occupancy levels at IOI Central Boulevard Towers and South Beach Tower.
"Complementing the growing property investment segment, the group's diversified product offerings across three countries (Malaysia, Singapore and China), the positive outlook of the hospitality and leisure segment and the favourable interest rate are anticipated to provide a strong foundation for sustained earnings for the Q4 of the financial year," he said in a separate statement.
No dividend was declared for the Q3 FY26.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: BCorp's net loss widens to RM176.23mil in Q3 FY26
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Berjaya Corp Bhd's (BCorp) net loss widened to RM176.23 million in the third quarter ended March 31, 2026 (Q3), compared with a net loss of RM92.34 million in the same period a year ago.
In a filing with Bursa Malaysia, BCorp said its revenue declined to RM2.19 billion in Q3 from RM2.54 billion previously.
The group said the higher net loss and lower revenue was mainly due to the performance and contributions from the services and non-food retail business segments.
The lower profit reported by the services segment was mainly attributable to the decline in revenue from the telecommunications network services (MTNS) business, while the lower profit from the non-food retail business was due to reduced revenue, coupled with higher statutory employment costs arising from the implementation of new United Kingdom labour regulations effective April 2025.
"The lower revenue reported by the non-food retail business was mainly attributed to lower sales contribution from HR Owen Plc, arising from lower sales volume in both the new and used car segments.
"The subdued performance of the new car sector was mainly attributed to extended vehicle product life cycles, which continued to constrain the model mix and availability of new models. In addition, when translated into ringgit, the revenue reduction was further impacted by unfavourable foreign exchange translation effects," it said.
It added that the decrease in MTNS revenue was mainly due to certain projects nearing the end of their deployment phase, with several projects having been completed in the previous financial year.
For the first nine months period, Berjaya registered a lower net loss of RM231.34 million from RM348.87 million previously, while its revenue decreased to RM6.70 billion compared with RM6.97 billion in the same period a year ago.
On prospects, BCorp said that the performance of the domestic business segments is expected to improve on the back of strong consumer spending and improvement in tourism activities.
"The directors are cautiously optimistic that the performance of the business operations of the group for the remaining quarter of the financial year ending June 30, 2026 (FY26) to be satisfactory," it said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Forex losses, highway provision drag IJM to RM173.89mil net loss in Q4
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: IJM Corp Bhd recorded a net loss of RM173.89 million for its fourth quarter ended March 2026 (Q4 FY26), compared to a net profit of RM128.95 million posted in the same period a year earlier, due to foreign exchange losses.
However, IJM's revenue for the quarter rose 4.2 per cent to RM1.86 billion from RM1.79 billion previously, according to its filing with Bursa Malaysia.
This was mainly supported by stronger contributions from its construction segment, as well as its manufacturing and quarrying divisions.
In Q4 FY26, IJM recorded net foreign exchange losses of RM94.7 million and recognised an impairment of RM121.6 million on unsold inventories, including an office building in Changchun, China.
A RM51 million provision for highway maintenance and resurfacing costs in India, coupled with weaker contributions from its property and port divisions, also weighed on earnings.
For the full financial year, IJM posted a lower net profit of RM3.24 million compared with RM403.37 million in the previous financial year, despite a 10 per cent increase in revenue to RM6.87 billion from RM6.25 billion.
This was due to unfavourable foreign exchange movements and non-cash one-off impairments undertaken to strengthen its balance sheet and improve future earnings visibility.
For FY26, the company declared a special dividend of one sen per share, in addition to the normal dividend of seven sen per share.
IJM group chief executive officer Datuk Lee Chun Fai said it is entering financial year 2027 with greater confidence, adding that the steps taken to optimise its portfolio in FY26 have positioned the company for a sustained earnings recovery.
"Our operations remain solid: our construction order book is at an all-time high, property sales have rebounded, our industry division continues to perform well, and our toll and port infrastructure portfolio are positioned for steady performance," he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: QL Resources posts lower net profit of RM450.35mil in FY26
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: QL Resources Bhd posted a lower net profit of RM450.35 million for the financial year ended March 31, 2026 (FY26), compared with RM455.08 million in the previous financial year.
Revenue was almost unchanged at RM7.047 billion, the integrated agro-based business group said in a Bursa Malaysia filing today.
The group also declared a final interim single-tier dividend of 2.5 sen per share for FY26.
For the fourth quarter ended March 31, 2026, QL Resources recorded a higher net profit of RM113.34 million versus RM93.40 million in the corresponding quarter last year.
It said revenue for the quarter increased to RM1.81 billion from RM1.74 billion previously, mainly due to stronger contributions from its marine product manufacturing (MPM) and integrated livestock farming (ILF) segments.
"For the current quarter, MPM's revenue increased by 10 per cent compared with the corresponding quarter, mainly driven by improved performance in fishing and aquaculture activities, alongside stable contribution from surimi-based products.
"This was partially offset by lower sales volumes of fishmeal and surimi, notwithstanding higher average selling prices," it said.
QL Resources said that despite weaker performance in fishmeal and surimi, cumulative profit before tax (PBT) decreased marginally, partially offset by a recovery in fishing and aquaculture activities and improved volumes and margins in surimi-based products.
As for ILF, the group said the segment's current-quarter revenue rose three per cent from the corresponding quarter previously, mainly driven by higher feed raw material trading volume despite lower unit prices.
"However, PBT decreased by 31 per cent, mainly attributable to the weaker performance of layer farming operations in Peninsular Malaysia arising from lower egg prices and the removal of the egg subsidy effective Aug 1, 2025.
"This was partially mitigated by a recovery in the Indonesian farming operations and stronger contributions from feed raw material trading," it said.
On prospects, QL Resources said the global economic outlook for 2026 remains uncertain amid ongoing trade fragmentation, the United States tariff policies and geopolitical tensions, particularly in West Asia.
"These factors are expected to persist in disrupting global supply chains, moderating investment flows and contributing to foreign exchange and energy price volatility," it said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Uribe's protege Valencia seeks to become Colombia's first female president
Article:
RIGHT-WING Colombian lawyer and senator Paloma Valencia is running to become her country's first female president in a Sunday presidential contest, promising to strengthen security and revive the economy, which she says have deteriorated under the current leftist government.
Valencia, 48, a scion of two powerful conservative families, is backed by former President Alvaro Uribe, who secured major victories against leftist guerrilla groups in the 2000s, and remains influential despite an ongoing legal case in which he was found guilty of fraud and bribery, convictions later overturned on appeal.
Uribe has denied wrongdoing, calling the case a political persecution.
"Uribe is like a father to me. I never make mistakes when it comes to loyalties. I want to take everything that worked in President Uribe's government and do it again," Valencia, who has recently slipped in polls to third, said at a recent campaign event.
"I'm going to copy Uribe, who got Colombia back on track."
Valencia is one of the leading candidates in the May 31 first-round vote to replace President Gustavo Petro, who is barred from seeking reelection. If no candidate wins more than 50 per cent of the vote, a runoff will be held in June.
One of the most high-profile opposition leaders in Congress, she hails from Cauca province, one of the regions hardest hit by violence and a six-decade armed conflict that has killed more than 450,000.
She has criticised the 2016 peace deal with the Revolutionary Armed Forces of Colombia (FARC) guerrillas, as well as Petro, who has unsuccessfully sought deals with other illegal armed groups.
"With me there will be no talks with the ELN, nor with the FARC, nor with the so-called Gaitanista Army.
"We will reactivate all arrest warrants and pursue them and hunt them down to bring them to prison," Valencia said at a recent event in Bogota, referencing other guerrilla and criminal groups.
Valencia's paternal grandfather was conservative former President Guillermo Leon Valencia, while her maternal grandfather founded a prestigious university.
She holds a master's degree in creative writing from New York University and was a newspaper columnist and a radio commentator before entering politics.
Her first electoral bid was in 2006, when she failed to win a congressional seat, but she has been a senator for Uribe's Democratic Center party since 2014, backing laws to support sugar producers, formalise small businesses and reduce working hours.
She is married to academic Tomas Rodriguez and has a young daughter.
* The writer is from Reuters
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Police record statements from seven in search of missing hiker
Article:
TAPAH: Police have recorded statements from seven people, including the husband of a woman who went missing while hiking at Gunung Batu Putih on May 23, to assist ongoing search and rescue (SAR) operations.
Deputy district police chief Deputy Superintendent Mohd Fadzil Ismail said the six others were mountain guides involved in the search for Jaslinda Saludin, 49, who has been missing for more than 72 hours and is believed to be without food and water.
"We are also sharing information with the Fire and Rescue Department. So far, developments have been positive," he told reporters after visiting the Fire and Rescue Control Post at Pos Kuala Woh here today.
Also present was Perak Fire and Rescue Department director Datuk Sayani Saidon.
Fadzil said the integrated and coordinated operation involving various agencies was expected to lead to Jaslinda's discovery.
He also urged the public not to speculate on the ongoing operation and appealed for space and privacy to be given to the victim's family.
Earlier reports stated that Jaslinda was among 14 hikers and two forestry mountain guides who began the Trans Spencer Chapman trail expedition at 2am on May 23 via the Pos Gedung-Gunung Bah Gading-Gunung Batu Putih-Kuala Woh route.
Jaslinda and another hiker, Mohd Hanafi Neikmad, 41, reportedly experienced health problems and stopped during the hike.
However, Jaslinda was later said to have continued the journey towards the mountain peak and was last seen by a forestry mountain guide at 7.30am on May 24.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Philippine senator charged over flood control scandal
Article:
MANILA: Philippine prosecutors filed criminal charges on Thursday against a senator allegedly involved in a massive corruption scandal over bogus flood control projects.
The charges came almost a year after President Ferdinand Marcos put the so-called "ghost" infrastructure projects, believed to have cost Filipino taxpayers billions of dollars, centre stage during a July speech.
Several construction firm owners, government officials and politicians have been accused of pocketing funds from the projects, but this is the first time a criminal case has been filed against a sitting lawmaker over the scandal.
On Thursday, Assistant Ombudsman Mico Clavano said the charges against Jose "Jinggoy" Estrada, a senator and son of former Philippine president Joseph Estrada, stemmed from "an intricate mechanism involving illegal budgetary insertions and project allocations" for fiscal year 2025 that allowed him to amass a kickback of over 573 million pesos (US$9.2 million).
The charges include graft and plunder, a non-bailable offence referring to massive corruption punishable by life imprisonment.
Estrada's co-respondents include former Department of Public Works and Highways secretary Manuel Bonoan, as well as local engineering officials.
Estrada did not immediately respond to an AFP request for comment.
"Our case is built on solid, immovable evidence," Clavano told reporters, adding that arrest warrants will be issued once the court finds probable cause.
"The Office of the Ombudsman is determined to pursue all cases still pending. We have one shot to get these cases right. The Filipino people need to see justice."
On Tuesday, Ombudsman Jesus Crispin Remulla said around eight more senators were being investigated and that three of them could also face charges. He did not provide their names.
Remulla said the president's congressman cousin Martin Romualdez, who resigned as a House Speaker in September after being implicated in the scandal, is also being investigated.
Last year, former congressman Elizaldy Co, public works officials and members of a construction firm were charged in connection to the scheme.
Corruption has long been a problem in the Philippines' brand of free-wheeling democracy, and accounts for much of the massive poverty in the Southeast Asian nation of 112 million people.
Estrada was charged in 2014 with plundering millions of dollars meant for government development projects, but the court acquitted him a decade later.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: DAP, Umno youth wings trade barbs over coalition loyalty
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: DAP and Umno Youth wings have traded barbs over political allegiances, with Umno Youth chief Datuk Dr Akmal Saleh saying DAP Youth should not forget that without Barisan Nasional, Pakatan Harapan might not have been able to form the federal government after the last general election.
"Don't get rattled when Umno starts talking about its strength. If you want to test strength, we can open the arena.
"Bring it on!" he said, challenging DAP Youth to prove that its political remarks were more than mere rhetoric.
The exchange comes after
DAP Youth
warned against attempts to undermine the unity government, saying parties within the coalition should remain committed to the mandate of stability and cooperation.
The call came as tensions continued to simmer between BN and PH following recent developments involving the Negri Sembilan government and heated exchanges over the upcoming Johor election.
BN is set to contest the Johor polls on its own.
Dr Akmal said DAP Youth appeared to have forgotten BN's role in helping PH form the federal government after the last general election.
He challenged the party to withdraw support from the Perak and Pahang governments if it truly wanted to test political strength.
"Don't just talk on social media. Make sure your leaders act on what you are calling for.
"Withdraw support in Perak. Withdraw support in Pahang. Let us fight in a gentlemanly manner and return the mandate to the people.
"DAP Youth should not cry out just because Umno Johor is talking about moving solo," he told the New Straits Times when contacted.
He said that there had never been any agreement requiring BN and PH to face future state or general elections together.
"I remind you, the government was formed today on the decree of the Yang di-Pertuan Agong for national stability after no coalition managed to secure a simple majority.
"That is the reality," he said.
He also dismissed accusations that Umno was betraying the unity government, saying BN had played a key role in ensuring political stability after the last general election.
DAP Youth also warned against attempts to weaken the
unity government
, saying coalition parties should remain committed to stability and cooperation.
It questioned moves by certain quarters in Umno to raise political tensions, particularly in Johor, despite holding senior positions in the unity government.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: GRS: Revised Special Grant rate for Sabah must take effect this year
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Gabungan Rakyat Sabah (GRS) remains firm on its stance that a revised Special Grant rate for Sabah under Articles 112C and 112D of the Federal Constitution must be implemented this year.
GRS secretary-general Datuk Armizan Mohd Ali said that despite ongoing legal proceedings at the Court of Appeal over the state's 40 per cent entitlement, he is confident the federal government will agree to review and increase the Special Grant rate in 2026.
In a social media post, Armizan attributed this to Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim's commitment in the Dewan Rakyat on Nov 13 last year, where the federal government recognised Sabah's 40 per cent Special Grant entitlement as enshrined in the Federal Constitution.
Armizan today attended a special discussion session on the implementation status of Sabah's 40 per cent entitlement rights at the Sabah International Convention Centre (SICC).
The session involved the top leadership of GRS and representatives from the Sabah State Legislative Assembly's Government Backbenchers Club (BBC), as well as the state's three deputy chief ministers who lead Sabah's delegation in the Technical Committee under the Malaysia Agreement 1963 (MA63) Action Council.
Armizan said the recognition reflected the present federal administration's commitment to correct the official stance taken by previous administrations, which had not only failed to conduct any review but had also previously informed parliament that Sabah's 40 per cent entitlement had ceased to apply.
"This commitment reflects the present federal government's determination to rectify the previous official position on Sabah's rights."
He added that GRS and the Sabah government under Chief Minister Datuk Seri Panglima Hajiji Noor would continue using existing negotiation platforms to ensure the revised Special Grant rate would be significantly increased in line with Sabah's constitutional rights and official recommendations submitted to Putrajaya.
The demand for the additional revenue is aimed at expanding Sabah's fiscal space to improve the people's wellbeing and accelerate the state's development.
Armizan said the increase in the Special Grant from only RM26.7 million annually between 1974 and 2021 to RM600 million a year currently had enabled the state government to implement several new initiatives.
Among them was the Rumah Mesra SMJ housing initiative, under which 6,634 houses had been completed and occupied by poor households from 2022 until April 30 this year.
He said the Sentuhan Kasih Rakyat (SYUKUR) programme, introduced two years ago, had also channelled monthly cash assistance to 140,000 heads of poor households statewide.
With the expected increase in the Special Grant this year, Armizan said the state government planned to focus on additional initiatives, including upgrading water piping networks to address non-revenue water (NRW) issues and constructing bridges statewide, particularly in rural areas.
He added that Sabah's claim for its 40 per cent entitlement was intended to ensure the additional revenue could be translated into tangible benefits for the people, especially vulnerable groups and rural communities.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: MARKET PULSE PM MAY 28, 2026 [WATCH]
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Bursa Malaysia ended lower on Thursday as investor sentiment turned increasingly risk-averse following renewed military strikes involving the US and Iran.
The benchmark index closed at 1,684.
The ringgit also weakened against the US dollar, closing at 3.9750.
Meanwhile, Bitcoin traded lower at RM291,454, while Ethereum also declined to RM7,905.
That wraps up today's Market Pulse.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Four men with combined 66 criminal offences nabbed after high-speed chase
Article:
PETALING JAYA: Four men with a combined 66 prior criminal offences were arrested after a high-speed chase ended with police intercepting their car at Jalan Air Jernih in Kuala Lumpur early today.
District police chief Assistant Commissioner Shamsuddin Mamat said the suspects, all in their 30s, had extensive criminal and drug-related records, with three of them also listed as wanted persons.
He said a Mobile Patrol Vehicle (MPV) unit spotted the suspects' vehicle at Persiaran Industri Kota Damansara at 2.23am and instructed the driver to stop for inspection.
"However, the suspects sped off and drove recklessly towards Air Panas, Semarak in Kuala Lumpur, before they were eventually intercepted at Jalan Air Jernih.
"All four men inside the vehicle were detained. A further search led to the discovery of various tools believed to be used for theft. These included a parang, a chisel, pliers, a bolt cutter, a crowbar, a screwdriver, and two rolls of ratchet straps," he said in a statement today.
Shamsuddin said checks revealed that the first suspect had 16 prior criminal offences, the second had 19, the third had 15, and the fourth had 16.
He added that all suspects have been remanded for two days until tomorrow, to assist in investigations under Section 186 of the Penal Code for obstructing a public servant from discharging their duties.
"Investigations are also being conducted under Section 28(i)(i) of the Minor Offences Act 1955 for possession of housebreaking tools," he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Ringgit ends lower against greenback as geopolitical concerns persist
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: The ringgit closed lower against the US dollar on Thursday, as escalating geopolitical tensions in the Middle East increased demand for the greenback amid heightened risk aversion.
At 6pm, the local currency eased to 3.9670/9770 versus the greenback from Tuesday's close of 3.9660/9705.
Bank Muamalat Malaysia Bhd chief economist Dr Mohd Afzanizam Abdul Rashid said the ongoing conflict involving Iran continued to dominate market sentiment after the United States (US) reportedly launched another attack on the country while Kuwait responded to missile and drone threats, signalling slimmer prospects for peace in the Gulf region.
He also noted that the heightened tensions pushed oil prices higher, with West Texas Intermediate (WTI) rising 2.21 per cent to US$90.64 per barrel and Brent crude increasing 2.26 per cent to US$96.42 per barrel.
"The US Dollar Index (DXY) rose 0.12 per cent to 99.329 points as risk-off sentiment became increasingly prevalent.
"As such, the ringgit remained on a weaker trajectory, ranging between RM3.9693 and RM3.9818 against the US dollar," Mohd Afzanizam told Bernama.
Meanwhile, he said risks of higher global inflation could lead markets to price in tighter monetary conditions, which are now reflected in higher bond yields.
"Therefore, market sentiment is expected to remain cautious in the near term," he added.
At the close, the ringgit traded higher against the British pound but eased versus the Japanese yen and the euro.
It appreciated vis-a-vis the British pound to 5.3272/3319 from 5.3470/3530 at Tuesday's close.
The local currency, however, slipped versus the Japanese yen to 2.4947/4970 from 2.4917/4947 on Tuesday and fell against the euro to 4.6181/6222 from 4.6172/6225 previously.
The ringgit traded mostly lower against regional peers.
It slid versus the Singapore dollar to 3.1085/1115 from 3.1038/1075 at Tuesday's close, declined against the Thai baht to 12.1613/1773 from 12.1485/1675 previously, and eased vis-a-vis the the Philippine peso at 6.45/6.46 against 6.44/6.46.
The ringgit was almost unchanged against the Indonesian rupiah at 222.8/223.1 versus 222.8/223.2 previously.
The market was closed on Wednesday, May 27, in conjunction with the Hari Raya Aidiladha public holiday.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Live snakes, dead bears and brain worms: RFK Jr's wild animal antics
Article:
WASHINGTON: A viral video of US Health Secretary Robert F. Kennedy Jr. picking up two writhing black snakes with his bare hands has put his often bizarre adventures with animals back in the spotlight.
Kennedy is an unusual government minister, surrounded by controversy over his fringe views that edge into conspiracy theories.
He's expressed skepticism about vaccines, falsely linking childhood immunizations to autism, and insisted that fluoride in public drinking water is unsafe.
And numerous stories have emerged about Kennedy's offbeat antics involving animals, accentuating his eccentric image.
In 2024, Kennedy acknowledged in a video that a decade earlier, he had put a dead bear cub in New York City's Central Park with a bicycle to make it look like a biking accident.
He said he had found the bear upstate after it had been hit by a car, and had put it in his vehicle to skin it, but then abandoned the plan.
The case mystified authorities for years.
According to a story recounted by his daughter, Kennedy also used a chainsaw to cut off the head of a dead whale that had washed ashore in Massachusetts.
He then strapped it to the roof of the family minivan to take it home to study its skull, she said.
Similarly, in a 2026 biography, Kennedy said he cut off the penis of a road-killed racoon to study it later.
[[nid:1450479]]
In another case, the New York Times reported that a doctor found a dead parasitic worm in Kennedy's brain after he had complained of memory loss.
He said he has recovered with no lasting impact.
None of these stories seem to embarrass Kennedy, the son of Robert F. Kennedy, who was assassinated in 1968 while running for president.
"He has a relationship with animals that most of us only dream of. Nightmares are also dreams," wrote Washington Post columnist Monica Hesse.
In the latest animal adventure, Kennedy on Tuesday posted a video of himself on X picking up two thin black snakes by their tails from the corner of an outdoor patio.
Wearing a tie and dress shirt, he holds up the writhing snakes and smiles.
At one point, he appears to be bitten by one of the snakes, while off camera a woman's voice says, "Bobby, Bobby, please."
"Cheryl cheerleads the removal of a pair of Black Racers from Dr Oz's patio," Kennedy's caption reads, an apparent reference to his wife, actress Cheryl Hines.
Mehmet Oz is the administrator of the Centers for Medicare and Medicaid Services, part of the health department Kennedy heads.
Black racers "are non-venomous and harmless to humans as long as they are left alone," according to the National Park Service.
Cameron Young, of the Center for Snake Conservation, warned that people should not pick them up as they will bite.
"What I don't want is people copying him," Young told AFP.
"If a kid picks up a venomous snake because RFK did, then the kid may receive a medically significant bite."
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Bursa Malaysia extends losses amid heightened geopolitical tensions
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Bursa Malaysia closed lower on Thursday, extending its losses for a third consecutive day, as investor sentiment turned increasingly risk-averse amid escalating geopolitical tensions.
At 5pm, the FTSE Bursa Malaysia KLCI (FBM KLCI) slipped 14.09 points, or 0.83 per cent, to 1,684.93 from Tuesday's close of 1,699.02.
The benchmark index opened 7.81 points higher at 1,706.83 and moved between 1,683.08 and 1,707.91 throughout the session.
Market breadth was negative, with losers outpacing gainers 678 to 456, while 550 counters were unchanged, 996 untraded, and 88 suspended.
Turnover rose to 3.53 billion units valued at RM4.19 billion compared with 3.24 billion units valued at RM3.72 billion on Tuesday.
IPPFA Sdn Bhd director of investment strategy and country economist Mohd Sedek Jantan said renewed military strikes involving the United States (US) and Iran raise concerns over a broader escalation and prolonged disruptions to global energy supply routes through the Strait of Hormuz.
He said following the attack, Brent crude oil climbed more than two per cent to around US$97 per barrel for August delivery as supply disruption fears resurfaced.
"Within the FBM KLCI, banking heavyweights led the market decline following the latest round of earnings releases, with investors adopting a more cautious stance amid concerns over margin compression, softer loan growth momentum, and rising external uncertainties.
"The weakness in financial serivices counters weighed significantly on the broader index, given the sector's large index representation," he told Bernama.
Among heavyweight stocks, Maybank fell 44 sen to RM10.50, Public Bank slipped three sen to RM4.77, CIMB was 19 sen lower at RM7.49, while Tenaga Nasional edged up eight sen to RM14.24, and  IHH Healthcare gained one sen to RM8.99.
As for the active stocks, Zetrix AI remained unchanged at 81.5 sen, OGX advanced seven sen to 42 sen, Inari Amertron garnered 13 sen to RM2.35, SFP Tech perked up 3.5 sen to 26 sen, while YTL Corp shed three sen to RM2.04.
Top gainers included Allianz Malaysia, which rose 50 sen to RM21.92, KESM Industries increased 47 sen to RM4.96, Ajinomoto firmed 32 sen to RM13.28, United Plantations added 30 sen to RM30.50, and Dutch Lady Milk climbed 24 sen to RM32.94.
Of the top losers, Hong Leong Bank tumbled 72 sen to RM21.18, Batu Kawan dropped 44 sen to RM19.96, Kuala Lumpur Kepong lost 38 sen to RM19.96, and Petronas Dagangan shaved off 30 sen to RM17.34.
On the index board, the FBM Emas Index fell 74.60 points to 12,541.28, the FBM Top 100 Index decreased 77.11 points to 12,380.48, the FBM Mid 70 Index eased 0.68 of a point to 18,213.69, while the FBM ACE Index edged up 9.65 points to 4,721.14, and the FBM Emas Shariah Index advanced 21.07 points to 12,550.91.
By sector, the Industrial Products and Services Index inched up 0.30 of a point to 198.33, while the Financial Services Index tumbled 422.94 points to 19,546.01, the Energy Index lost 3.91 points to 782.90, and the Plantation Index dipped 30.33 points to 8,504.95.
The Main Market volume improved to 1.98 billion units valued at RM3.87 billion from 1.78 billion units valued at RM3.41 billion on Tuesday.
Warrants turnover slid to 971.39 million units valued at RM117.20 million from 981.14 million units valued at RM133.62 million previously.
The ACE Market volume expanded to 583.66 million units valued at RM206.61 million from 471.50 million units valued at RM167.37 million on Tuesday.
Consumer products and services counters accounted for 263.32 million shares traded on the Main Market, industrial products and services (367.03 million), construction (152.51 million), technology (380.56 million), financial services (121.22 million), property (144.16 million), plantation (41.69 million), real estate investment trusts (19.77 million), closed-end fund (19,300), energy (165.95 million), healthcare (104.73 million), telecommunications and media (99.77 million), transportation and logistics (29.36 million), utilities (88.65 million), and business trusts (508,300).
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Body of missing 10-year-old boy recovered from Sungai Bernam
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: A 10-year-old boy feared drowned after being swept away by strong currents in Sungai Bernam near Kampung Sungai Nilam in Hulu Selangor was found dead this evening.
Selangor Fire and Rescue Department Zone 6 chief Mustakim Rimon said the body of Muhamad Aakif Imran Asrul Nizam was recovered at 4.36pm, about 300 metres from the spot where he was believed to have fallen into the river yesterday.
He said the Water Rescue Team (PPDA) conducted a dive operation in waters estimated to be six metres deep before retrieving the victim from the riverbed.
"The victim was later handed over to the police for further action," he said in a statement today.
The search and rescue operation, which
entered its second day today
, involved surface searches within a three-kilometre radius from the scene.
Mustakim said the operation involved personnel from the Slim River Fire and Rescue Station, PPDA and the K9 Unit, with assistance from the police, Health Ministry, volunteer fire brigade and the Hulu Selangor District Office.
In the incident yesterday, the department received an emergency call at 1.40pm before personnel from the Kuala Kubu Baru Fire and Rescue Station, assisted by the Tanjung Malim station, were deployed to the scene.
Hulu Selangor police chief Assistant Commissioner Ibrahim Husin said initial investigations found that the victim was believed to have fallen into the river while playing along the riverbank before being swept away by strong currents.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Heavy rain, strong winds expected in several states until 9pm
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: The Malaysian Meteorological Department (MetMalaysia) has issued a thunderstorm, heavy rain and strong winds warning for seven states until 9pm today.
In the warning issued at 5pm, MetMalaysia said the adverse weather conditions were expected to affect Perak, involving Kerian, Larut, Matang and Selama, Hulu Perak, Kuala Kangsar, Kinta and Kampar.
The warning was also issued for Negri Sembilan involving Jelebu, Kuala Pilah, Jempol and Tampin, while in Johor, the affected areas are Segamat and Mersing.
In Terengganu, the affected areas are Kuala Nerus, Hulu Terengganu, Kuala Terengganu, Marang, Dungun and Kemaman, while in Pahang, the warning covers Jerantut, Temerloh, Maran, Kuantan, Bera and Rompin.
Meanwhile, in Sarawak, the affected areas are Kapit (Kapit and Bukit Mabong), Bintulu (Tatau and Sebauh) and Limbang, while in Sabah, the warning involves Kinabatangan in Sandakan.
– Bernama
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Tan Chong swings into the red in Q1
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Tan Chong Motor Holdings Bhd posted a net loss of RM12.18 million in the first quarter ended March 31, 2026 (Q1 FY26) from a net profit of RM4.14 million a year ago.
Its revenue for the quarter under review dropped 17.2 per cent to RM457.77 million from RM553 million in the same period last year.
In a filing with Bursa Malaysia, the company said that the loss was mainly due to one-off fair value gain on investment properties of RM54 million recognised in Q1 FY25.
As at March 31, the company's retained earnings stood at RM1.15 billion.
The automotive division recorded lower revenue of RM439.4 million in the current quarter under review, down 17.2 per cent year-on-year (YoY). Pre-tax earnings dropped 92.1 per cent YoY to RM4.5 million.
Additionally, the financial services division recorded revenue of RM14.8 million in Q1 FY26, down RM3.3 million YoY.
Moving forward, the company said strategic initiatives undertaken over the past year are anticipated to contribute more meaningfully in the coming quarters.
"The encouraging response towards the electric vehicle, TQ Wuling Bingo, supports the group's broader multi-brand and electrification strategy, while the recent launch of the All-New Nissan Serena e-Power will further strengthen the group's product offering and support sales momentum for the Nissan brand.
"These products are geared for consumers seeking cost effective mobility solutions compared to conventional engines,"  it said.
It added that management will continue to focus on cost discipline, operational efficiency, prudent capital management and liquidity preservation.
"While challenges relating to competition, geopolitical developments and cost pressures remain, management remains cautiously optimistic that the group is better positioned to navigate the evolving market environment and progressively strengthen its performance in the remaining quarters of 2026."
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: [UPDATED] Teen girl allegedly under influence of drug-laced vape slashes neck, wrist
Article:
MELAKA: A 14-year-old girl, allegedly under the influence of a drug-laced vape, slashed her neck and wrist after her foster mother stopped her from meeting her boyfriend at a house in Jonker Walk here.
Melaka police chief Datuk Dzulkhairi Mukhtar said the incident occurred at 10.33pm last Sunday when the teenager became enraged after her 49-year-old foster mother barred her from seeing her 17-year-old boyfriend.
He said the girl then locked herself in a first-floor bedroom before injuring herself with a knife.
"The foster mother claimed the teenager was under severe distress and believed to be under the influence of a 'mushroom' vape before the incident.
"The victim was rushed to the Melaka Hospital for treatment in the Emergency Unit, where her condition has since been reported as stable," he said in a statement today.
Dzulkhairi said investigations found that the girl was first introduced to the vape liquid in February by her boyfriend, who is a Form Four student.
He said the foster mother had previously confiscated the vape device to prevent further use, adding that the substance was believed to have been supplied by the boyfriend.
"Further investigations are ongoing involving all individuals connected to the case, including the foster mother and other occupants of the house.
"The case is being investigated under Section 31(1)(a) of the Child Act 2001 for suspected negligence or abandonment of a child. Upon conviction, offenders can face up to 10 years in prison, a maximum fine of RM20,000, or both," he said.
It was previously reported on Tuesday that the teenager was receiving treatment at Melaka Hospital after she was found with slash wounds on her neck at a house in Banda Hilir.
State Health, Human Resources and Unity Committee chairman Datuk Ngwe Hee Sem reportedly said the victim was in stable condition and remained under medical observation.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Environmental offences: DoE imposes RM4.59mil in fines, 148 convictions
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: The Department of Environment (DoE) carried out 5,074 inspections nationwide, covering 4,339 premises, between Jan 1 and April 30 this year as part of intensified enforcement under the Environmental Quality Act 1974.
The department said the inspections covered various high-risk industries, including crude palm oil mills, raw rubber factories, scheduled waste recovery facilities and sewage treatment plants.
In a statement today, the DoE said the nationwide operations also involved inspections of industrial sectors, development projects, motor vehicle emissions, environmental complaints and monitoring to prevent open burning incidents.
"In ensuring environmental sustainability and the well-being of the people, the DoE will continue strengthening enforcement of the Environmental Quality Act 1974 in a firm and integrated manner," it said.
It said 1,906 enforcement operations were also conducted on motor vehicle emissions involving inspections of 137,722 vehicles nationwide.
The operations resulted in compounds, prohibition orders and 764 retest directives being issued to ensure vehicles complied with emission standards.
On development monitoring, the department conducted 811 inspections on projects that had obtained approval for Environmental Impact Assessment (EIA) reports.
The DoE also said it had obtained MS ISO 9001:2015 certification for the scope of EIA report processing and enforcement of EIA conditions until 2028.
As part of efforts to curb the illegal entry of scheduled waste into the country, the department conducted 51 inspection operations at major ports involving checks on 702 containers suspected of carrying scheduled waste.
"Inspections found that 427 containers contained scheduled waste, including electrical and electronic waste (e-waste).
"A total of 73 directive notices were issued to order the containers to be shipped out of Malaysia, while 53 containers have so far been successfully repatriated to their countries of origin," it said.
The department said it also intensified monitoring of open burning activities, with 3,237 investigations conducted nationwide, including 494 hotspots suspected of open burning activities.
A total of 409 drone flights were also carried out to strengthen environmental monitoring and assist enforcement investigations more effectively.
The DoE said it received 6,252 environmental pollution complaints during the first four months of the year, adding that every complaint was investigated according to the stipulated client charter period.
"Based on inspections and investigations conducted, the department took 3,149 follow-up enforcement actions against parties found to have breached the Environmental Quality Act 1974.
"These included 1,587 directive notices, 1,369 compounds, 44 equipment operation detention actions, two licence suspension actions, 18 prohibition orders and 129 investigation papers opened for prosecution purposes," it said.
The DoE added that 148 offences under the Environmental Quality Act 1974 had been prosecuted and convicted in court, resulting in total fines amounting to RM4,598,500 and a one-day jail sentence.
The department stressed that it would not compromise with any party responsible for environmental pollution or non-compliance with environmental laws.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: 11,000 pigs remain as Tanjong Sepat farm closures near completion
Article:
KUALA LANGAT: The closure of pig farms in Tanjong Sepat is in its final phase, with only about 11,000 pigs remaining at farms still operating in stages, the Selangor government said today.
Selangor Infrastructure and Agriculture Committee chairman Datuk Izham Hashim said the closure involved about 30 farms following eviction orders issued earlier this year.
He said the farms were allowed to continue operating temporarily because some of the livestock were not yet mature enough for disposal when the order was issued in February.
"No new piglets are being brought in and all livestock will be slaughtered at the slaughterhouse in Rawang. The process is being carried out in stages and we expect it to be completed soon.
"The menteri besar (Datuk Seri Amirudin Shari), deputy agriculture and food security minister Datuk Chan Foong Hin and I recently held a meeting.
"The transition is important to ensure high-value agricultural activities can be carried out on the 141.6ha site," he said after attending the Pertubuhan Wartawan dan Jurugambar Selangor (Wajar) sacrificial ritual programme at Surau Haji Hassan in Sijangkang today.
Izham said another meeting would be held on June 9 to review federal and state government assistance measures, including land restoration efforts and support for the 87 farm operators affected.
He said the assistance aimed to help operators transition into other suitable agricultural activities once livestock operations cease completely.
Earlier, Tanjong Sepat assemblyman Datuk Borhan Aman Shah said all pig farms in the area would be fully shut down by the end of June before enforcement begins on July 1.
He also said the state government had given operators six months to wind down operations, including selling off their livestock.
The move followed a decree by the Sultan of Selangor, Sultan Sharafuddin Idris Shah, who has opposed pig farming activities in any district in Selangor.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Press Metal's earnings jumps 35pct in Q1, declares 2.5 sen dividend
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Press Metal Aluminium Holdings Bhd's saw a 35.2 per cent jump in earnings to RM624.5 million in the first quarter ended March 31, 2026 from RM461.77 million a year ago.
Revenue for the quarter under review rose 5.2 per cent to RM4.1 billion from RM3.9 billion last year.
The company said that the improved performance was driven mainly by higher metal prices and increased sales volume during the quarter.
Despite lower contributions from associate companies, the company delivered a significantly stronger bottom line on the back of lower raw material costs.
Group chief executive officer Tan Sri Paul Koon said the significant supply shock caused by the Middle East conflict is having far reaching consequences on market equilibrium, driving aluminium prices and premiums higher while reshaping regional metal trade flows.
"However, supply chain disruptions and freight cost volatility have resurfaced, creating upward pressure on logistics costs in the coming months.
"Meanwhile, macroeconomic uncertainty arising from geopolitical risks, supply disruptions and inflationary pressures continues to weigh on global end-use consumption and overall demand sentiments," he said.
He added that the company will continue to closely monitor developments in the global market while remaining focused on operational resilience, disciplined execution and capturing growth opportunities as they arise.
Press Metal declared a first interim dividend of 2.5 sen per share for the quarter, representing a payout ratio of 33 per cent. The dividend will be paid on June 30.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: YTL Corp Q3 revenue rises to RM7.57bil, profit weighed by weaker power generation
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: YTL Corp Bhd posted higher revenue for the third quarter ended March 31, 2026, but lower profit, mainly due to weaker contributions from its power generation business within the utilities division.
Revenue rose to RM7.57 billion from RM7.32 billion a year earlier, while pre-tax profit fell to RM861.6 million from RM987.9 million. Net profit also eased to RM629.2 million from RM744.5 million.
Executive chairman Tan Sri (Sir) Francis Yeoh Sock Ping said the revenue growth was supported by stronger performance from the cement and building materials, hotel operations and utilities segments. However, profit was impacted by lower earnings from power generation.
For the nine-month period, revenue fell slightly to RM22.8 billion from RM23.15 billion, while net profit declined to RM2.08 billion from RM2.41 billion a year earlier.
YTL Power International Bhd, the group's power arm, recorded higher quarterly revenue of RM5.08 billion versus RM4.9 billion previously, but pre-tax profit dropped to RM442.7 million from RM635.2 million, while net profit declined to RM342 million from RM508.2 million.
The company declared an interim dividend of four sen per share for the financial year ending June 30, 2026.
Yeoh said the weaker power generation performance was due to lower retail and vesting margins, as well as currency movements, while the water segment in the UK and Malaysia posted significantly stronger results, supported by regulatory tariff increases.
For the nine months, the UK water business recorded a 472 per cent jump in pre-tax profit, while Malaysia's water operations rose 126 per cent. Meanwhile, the data centre segment saw a 422 per cent growth in pre-tax profit compared to the same period last year.
YTL Power's data centre and AI businesses also delivered strong growth, with the newly reported segment posting a pre-tax profit of RM59 million on revenue of RM225 million in the quarter and RM101 million profit on RM408 million revenue for the nine months, Yeoh said.
He said with 298 megawatt (MW) in data centre capacity already contracted, YTL Power is planning to increase the capacity of its YTL Green Data Centre Park to 1,000 MW from the existing 600 MW.
Yeoh added that the park will be co-powered by solar energy, and the first phase involving a 215 MWac solar power generation facility is currently being implemented.
"The group has invested RM8.1 billion in the data centre business to date, and we are looking to expand geographically beyond the YTL Green Data Centre Park in Kulai to further grow this business," he said.
In artificial intelligence, YTL AI Cloud has been certified as an Nvidia Exemplar Cloud Provider, while its ILMU large language model ecosystem has expanded to include ILMUChat, ILMU Claw and ILMU Coding. The group said the models are designed to be locally relevant, with Bahasa Melayu capability and regional context awareness.
"We believe this AI space is one in which Malaysia can be a regional leader," Yeoh said.
For the cumulative nine-month period ended March 31, 2026, YTL Power's revenue stood at RM15.7 billion, compared with RM16.25 billion recorded in the corresponding period a year earlier, while profit after tax declined to RM1.29 billion from RM1.79 billion previously.
Meanwhile, Malayan Cement Bhd, another key subsidiary, posted stronger earnings, with revenue rising 12 per cent to RM1.22 billion and net profit increasing 35 per cent to RM247 million for the quarter.
Yeoh said growth was driven by higher demand for ready-mixed and dry-mix products, while profitability was supported by cost efficiencies, automation, renewable energy adoption and waste heat recovery initiatives, despite higher transport costs.
For the nine months, Malayan Cement's revenue rose 8 per cent to RM3.70 billion, while net profit increased 34 per cent to RM681 million.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: DAP Youth warns against attempts to destabilise unity government
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: DAP Youth has warned against attempts to undermine the unity government, saying parties within the coalition should remain committed to the mandate of stability and cooperation.
DAP Youth questioned what it described as efforts by certain quarters within Umno to escalate political tensions, particularly in Johor, despite holding senior positions in the unity government.
The youth wing said Pakatan Harapan (PH) had played a significant role in helping Barisan Nasional (BN) remain politically relevant following the coalition's poor performance in the last general election and the 2023 state polls.
It said cooperation and seat negotiations between PH and BN during the state elections had prevented clashes between coalition partners and enabled Umno to retain influence in several states.
DAP Youth also pointed to PH's support for Umno leaders to become menteri besar in Perak and Pahang as part of efforts to maintain political stability.
"However, DAP Youth sees certain parties behaving like 'kacang lupakan kulit' (forgetting those who helped them)," it said in a statement.
The wing also criticised what it described as attempts to destabilise the Negri Sembilan government and rhetoric calling for an "all-out war" in Johor.
DAP Youth cited comments by Umno Supreme Council member Lokman Adam and views by political analysts who argued that Umno would struggle to defeat Perikatan Nasional (PN) independently in Malay-Muslim majority constituencies.
It said Umno continued to require support from urban and non-Malay voters, which could only be secured through stability, moderation and good governance.
The youth wing also referred to projections by electoral reform group Tindak Malaysia suggesting PH could potentially form the Johor government if a general election and state election were held simultaneously.
DAP Youth said if there were parties within the coalition seeking to "stab from behind" and undermine the spirit of unity, Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim should consider dissolving Parliament to allow a general election and Johor state election to be held concurrently with polls in other states.
It said such a move would allow voters to make a "clear and final decision".
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Sombre Aidiladha for family of injured Gaza activist Razman warded in Turkiye
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: It was a sombre Hari Raya Aidiladha for the family of Gaza aid mission volunteer and activist Razman Mat Ali, who is currently receiving medical treatment in Istanbul, Turkiye.
"My four children, aged four to 10, understood and supported their father's humanitarian mission. They are proud of him for wanting to help the people of Gaza.
"They always video call him and constantly tell him how much they miss him," Razman's wife, Afifah Yusof, 37, told the New Straits Times from her home in Bayan Lepas, Penang.
She added that it was a modest
Aidiladha celebration for the family without Razman
.
"We celebrated as usual by performing Aidiladha prayers at home. The children also went to watch the korban (slaughter of livestock) at the mosque.
"Usually, my husband would roast lemang during Hari Raya, but this year it was not as lively as previous celebrations," she said.
Razman, who suffered a punctured lung after allegedly being assaulted by Israeli soldiers during the humanitarian mission to Gaza, remains under close medical observation.
Afifah said doctors had initially planned to carry out a second operation yesterday or today following complications linked to his injuries.
However, after a video call with her husband this morning, she learned the surgery had been postponed as his condition showed signs of improvement.
"Alhamdulillah, with everyone's prayers, the second surgery did not proceed. But doctors are still monitoring his condition closely, and if the pain worsens, they may still carry out the operation," she said.
Afifah added that Razman may remain warded for observation until the end of June.
"The earliest he can return is by the end of next month, and even then, it may require an air ambulance to bring him back.
"My husband still cannot board a normal commercial flight home," she said, adding that doctors had initially expected to remove his chest tube before discovering lingering internal issues.
She said Razman also complained of sharp pain after sneezing.
Afifah added that while the family is considering traveling to Turkiye to offer him bedside support, discussions are still ongoing as the children are still in school and his discharge date remains uncertain.
"InshaAllah, we will go there to give him support, but we do not have a set date yet," she said.
Afifah also condemned the ongoing atrocities in Gaza.
"We always discuss the cruelty committed by Israel and what the people of Gaza are going through. It makes us feel that our hardships are much smaller compared to the immense suffering endured by Palestinians," she said.
On May 25, Sumud Nusantara Command Centre director-general Datuk Sani Araby Abdul Alim Araby confirmed that Razman had suffered a traumatic pneumothorax, or a punctured lung, caused by an assault by Israeli soldiers during the interception of their convoy.
Twenty-eight Malaysian volunteers
from the Global Sumud Flotilla (GSF) 2.0 mission have since returned home after being detained by Israeli forces while attempting to deliver humanitarian aid to Gaza.
Razman
was the only volunteer unable to return with the delegation due to his medical condition.
The GSF 2.0 mission, carrying 430 activists from various countries, including 29 Malaysians, departed from Barcelona Port on April 12.
The automated tracking and communications for the 50-ship convoy were cut off after they were intercepted and detained by Israeli forces on May 18.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Man sentenced to death for brutal abuse and murder of child
Article:
KLANG: A lorry driver was sentenced to death by the Klang High Court for murdering the 3-year-old son of a single mother he met online in 2021.
Judge Norliza Othman imposed the sentence on S. Davandakumar after ruling that death by hanging was the only appropriate and proportionate punishment for the brutal killing of K. Nithish.
In her 32-page written judgment, Norliza said she arrived at her decision after examining the post-mortem findings, which revealed the extent of abuse suffered by the frail child.
"The evidence showed that the child was mercilessly assaulted by the accused without compassion. The child, being unable to defend himself, could only cry in pain," she said.
She said the deceased was subjected to repeated abuse, including being struck with a chair, belt and clothes hanger, bitten on the neck and having his head submerged in water by the accused.
"The child was dragged into a room where he was further assaulted. The mother could only hear her child's cries from outside a locked room before finding him lifeless," she said.
She said the post-mortem findings clearly reflected the severe suffering endured by the child before his death.
"The court can only conclude that the accused's actions were not merely homicidal but were also cruel and inhumane towards a helpless child incapable of defending himself.
"Accordingly, the only appropriate and proportionate sentence for such conduct is death by hanging," she said.
Davandakumar was charged with murdering Nithish between 9.30am and 5.30pm at a house in Taman Sentosa, Klang on Dec 24, 2021.
The court heard that Davandakumar had met the child's mother, M. Sutha, who is from Seremban, and later moved in with her together with her son.
Sutha testified that the accused initially treated both her and her son well. However, by the third week of living together, the abuse began.
She told the court that on Dec 22, the accused assaulted the boy using a stick taken from a broken chair as well as a belt.
She said she tried to stop the accused but was also assaulted in the process.
The next day, she said she was bathing the boy when the accused barged in and began beating him while questioning why the child could not bathe himself.
She said the accused then struck the boy with a rubber hose and bit him on the neck.
The accused later took the child to the toilet, held him upside down and submerged his head in a pail of water for about five minutes while continuing to assault him.
She told the court that the accused then threw the boy onto a bed and locked the room.
When the accused finally opened the door, the mother saw the boy lying motionless on the bed, although he was still breathing.
They initially brought the child to a clinic but were told to take him to Tengku Ampuan Rahimah Hospital.
However, by then, the boy had already died. His cause of death was identified as massive soft tissue injury due to blunt trauma.
A pathologist testified that Nithish had been subjected to extremely violent treatment by the accused and that the injuries inflicted were far beyond what a child of his age could tolerate, resulting in his death.
The judge said the mother's testimony was credible and reliable, noting that she was the sole eyewitness to the brutal abuse inflicted on the child and had consistently recounted and reenacted the sequence of events during her testimony.
The court also applied the doctrine of "last seen together" in evaluating the chain of circumstances leading to the child's death, noting that the accused was the last person seen with the boy before he was found unconscious.
Deputy public prosecutor Farah Aqilah Ahmad Fuad prosecuted, while the accused was represented by Messrs Zamri & Teoh.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Terrain constraints prevent K9 deployment in search for missing hiker
Article:
TAPAH: The Fire and Rescue Department has not deployed its K9 unit in the search and rescue (SAR) operation for missing hiker Jaslinda Saludin, 49, at Gunung Batu Putih due to the dangerous terrain and vertical rescue limitations.
Perak Fire and Rescue Department director Datuk Sayani Saidon said deploying tracking dogs into the densely forested area would require them to be lowered by helicopter winching, posing significant operational risks.
"We have considered using the K9 unit, but inserting the dogs into the search area is extremely difficult, as even our personnel have to be winched down.
"Under these conditions, deploying K9 assets is not practical for now," she said at the fire control centre at Surau At-Taqwa Sultan Idris II Woh Power Station here today.
Sayani said the SAR operation is now focused on Sungai Air Busuk, as rescuers believe the victim may head toward the river for water since her emergency rations are likely depleted.
Given that the search has surpassed the critical 72-hour mark and Jaslinda has highly limited camping gear, rescuers suspect her physical condition may be deteriorating rapidly.
"I was informed by the mountain guides that the victim did not carry adequate food, only dates and two bottles of water. Remaining energetic after 72 hours under these conditions is difficult, especially since she lacks proper gear to shield herself from the cold.
"Her best chance of survival would be finding her way to Kem Sukaneka if she knows there is a water source at Sungai Air Busuk. This is also her second time climbing Gunung Batu Putih," she said.
Sayani added that if the victim seeks out Sungai Air Busuk via Kem Sukaneka, it could take her about three days to make her way down toward Pos Musuh, taking into account her weakened physical state and the steep, rapid-filled river terrain.
Meanwhile, Tapah deputy district police chief Deputy Superintendent Mohd Fadzil Ismail said 92 personnel from various agencies have been mobilised for the operation.
The agencies include the police, the Fire and Rescue Department, the General Operations Force (GOF), the Forestry Department, and forestry mountain guides.
He said ground search teams are pushing out from Pos Musuh and Pos Kuala Woh in smaller, specialised groups of three to four personnel, accompanied by experienced mountain guides familiar with the treacherous topography.
"A team of 18 personnel, including an officer from the GOF, will partner with the mountain guides and the Forestry Department to move in by land, while the fire department coordinates from the air.
"On the ground, we start at Pos Kuala Woh. Once the main team reaches a designated staging area, we will split into smaller teams of three to four people," he said.
Jaslinda
, alongside 13 others and two mountain guides, commenced the Trans Spencer Chapman trek at 2am on May 23 via the Pos Gedung-Gunung Bah Gading-Gunung Batu Putih-Kuala Woh route.
She and another climber, Mohd Hanafi Neikmad, 41, experienced health issues and fell behind.
Jaslinda reportedly pushed on toward the summit alone and was last seen by a mountain guide at 7.30am last Sunday.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: UN condemns 'dangerous escalation' in Ukraine war
Article:
GENEVA: The United Nations rights chief warned Thursday against a "dangerous escalation" in Ukraine, and Russia's threats to ramp up attacks, urging both sides to return to the negotiating table.
"I strongly urge restraint. Resume negotiations and end the suffering," Volker Turk said in a statement.
His appeal comes days after one of the worst combined missile and drone attacks on Kyiv since Russia launched its full-scale invasion of Ukraine more than four years ago.
It was the latest in a string of large-scale attacks by Russia in recent weeks.
The UN rights office said 815 civilians had been killed and 4,174 injured in Ukraine in the first four months of 2026 – a 21 per cent increase in civilian casualties over the same period last year.
"As if all these casualty figures weren't horrifying enough on their own, following these attacks, Russian officials have publicly threatened to increase attacks across Kyiv," Turk said.
"International humanitarian law demands that parties to a conflict take all feasible precautions to avoid civilian harm," he said.
"These are not simply suggestions or recommendations, but binding obligations carrying legal responsibility for those involved."
His office also pointed to an attack by Ukrainian armed forces on an educational complex in the occupied city of Starobilsk on May 21-22, in which Russian authorities say 21 people were killed and 44 injured.
"The UN Human Rights Office has conducted a thorough review of publicly-available information, which indicates that the educational facilities were operational at the time of the attack and that civilians – many of them students – were killed or injured," it said.
Eighteen of those killed were women, it said, adding that attacks by Ukrainian armed forces had also killed and injured civilians within Russia itself.
Turk called on both Russian and Ukrainian authorities to conduct "prompt, independent, and effective investigations and hold those responsible accountable."
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Hassan: PKR 'injured and wounded', risks collapse if not treated
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: PKR is now "injured, wounded and severely hurt", with "a lot of blood flowing out", and could face paralysis or even collapse if it is not treated immediately, said Hassan Abdul Karim.
The Pasir Gudang member of parliament said he was saddened by the current state of the party amid an alleged wave of departures involving members joining Parti Bersama Malaysia, led by former PKR deputy president Datuk Seri Rafizi Ramli.
Hassan said he was now only a backbencher on PKR's fringes, choosing silence as he alleged many senior PKR lawmakers had lost confidence in the party while only a handful still firmly backed party president and Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim.
"In Parliament, it is just me and a few other backbenchers that still support Anwar and PKR.
"I see PKR now as injured, wounded and severely hurt. A lot of blood is flowing out.
"If it is not treated immediately, it could become paralysed or even die," he said in a Facebook post today.
He said the internal turmoil allegedly gripping PKR now reminded him of the political crisis during the Sheraton Move in 2020, when several PKR lawmakers left the party to support Tan Sri Muhyiddin Yassin and Bersatu.
"At that time, I tried to persuade PKR lawmakers not to leave the party and not to betray the struggle, but my efforts failed," he said.
He said the defections led to the collapse of the first Pakatan Harapan government, but admitted he no longer had the strength to fight the tide or save PKR from its current crisis.
Hassan said he still hoped PKR would survive the alleged internal rebellion.
He said the situation was a test of democracy and that PKR's future would likely be determined in the 16th general election (GE16).
"If PKR survives the next general election, then it still has a future. If it wins convincingly, then PMX will be prime minister once again," he said.
However, Hassan said if PKR was defeated at the ballot box, then all party leaders and members would need to reflect deeply on themselves, although he stressed there was still time for the party to prepare itself before the next polls.
PKR is facing a critical period after Rafizi and its former vice-president Nik Nazmi Nik Ahmad announced that they had taken over
Bersama
.
The duo had previously held the Pandan and Setiawangsa parliamentary seats, respectively, before
vacating
them.
Rafizi has claimed Bersama received 18,000
membership applications
within a week, with 29 per cent coming from PKR.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Putrajaya and Selangor must act fast to ‘lock in’ proposed PJ public hospital, says Kampung Tunku rep
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR, May 28 — Kampung Tunku assemblyman Lim Yi Wei today urged Putrajaya and the Selangor government to move quickly on plans for a new public hospital in Petaling Jaya, warning that delays in land matters and funding could derail the project.
Welcoming the recent announcement of a finalised site for the proposed hospital, Lim said the land should be immediately rezoned as a hospital site under the “Institutional and Public Facilities Zone”.
“To avoid a ‘rinse and repeat’, land administration matters must be quickly negotiated and executed so that the project is fully locked in,” she said in a statement.
Lim also called for the project to be formally included under Budget 2027 and the 13th Malaysia Plan, saying clear fiscal commitments were needed to ensure the hospital becomes a reality.
She said interim measures should also be introduced to ease healthcare access for Petaling Jaya residents while the hospital is being developed, including accelerating talks to include University Malaya Medical Centre under Selangor’s RM10,000 hospitalisation benefit scheme.
“Healthcare must remain a public good: undisturbed by austerity and protected from profiteering,” she said.
Lim also warned that building a new hospital alone would not resolve deeper structural problems in the healthcare system.
“If we don’t shed the austerity mindset or show willpower to address understaffing, fair pay, overwork, or congestion, a new public hospital for PJ may still struggle to meet residents’ healthcare needs.”

Title: One wild ‘Odyssey’: PJ police nab four suspected thieves after car chase from Kota Damansara to Air Panas
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR, May 28 — Four local men were arrested after driving dangerously and fleeing from a police patrol car from Kota Damansara to Air Panas here early this morning.
Petaling Jaya district police chief ACP Shamsudin Mamat said the incident began at 2.23am when members of a police patrol car tried to inspect a black Honda Odyssey multi-purpose vehicle (MPV).
He said that during the incident, the vehicle carrying all the suspects was spotted in suspicious circumstances at Persiaran Industri Kota Damansara near a petrol station.
“However, the suspect refused to stop the vehicle after being instructed to do so. Instead, they fled and drove aggressively towards the city centre.
“The police’s swift action, however, succeeded in intercepting the vehicle at Jalan Air Jernih, Air Panas, Kuala Lumpur before arresting the four suspects,” he said in a statement today.
Shamsudin said an inspection of the vehicle found various equipment suspected of being used for theft and housebreaking.
Among the items seized were a machete, chisel, iron cutter, goat claw, screwdriver and ratchet.
Commenting on the background of the suspects, who are in their 30s, he said checks found that they had between 15 and 19 previous criminal records involving various offences, with three of them listed as wanted individuals.
He said all the suspects were remanded for two days until tomorrow and the case was being investigated, among others, under Section 186 of the Penal Code for obstructing a public servant in the performance of his duties and Section 6(1) of the Corrosive and Explosive Substances and Offensive Weapons Act 1958. — Bernama

Title: Missing Gunung Batu Putih hiker may be searching for water after 72 hours, says Perak Bomba chief
Article:
TAPAH, May 28 — The condition of female hiker, Jaslinda Saludin, 49, who went missing while climbing Gunung Batu Putih here on May 23, has entered the critical phase after being lost for over 72 hours.
Perak Fire and Rescue Department (JBPM) director Datuk Sayani Saidon said that in the face of this, the search and rescue operation has been intensified, with more personnel deployed and the search area expanded to areas where the victim may look for food and water.
The search will now focus on the area along Sungai Ayer Busok, where the victim may have gone to find water.
“We can say that this is a critical period, but given the victim’s experience, she should be able to survive while the SAR operation is ongoing.
“With her experience, we believe she will attempt to find a water source to sustain herself, and is highly likely to head towards Sungai Ayer Busok, which is close to Gunung Batu Putih,” she told a press conference after visiting the incident command post at Pos Kuala Poh here today.
Sayani said 36 personnel from JBPM and the Royal Malaysia Police (PDRM) were deployed today to the Kem Sukaneka and Gunung Rayu areas, assisted by the General Operations Force (GOF), the Forestry Department, and Forestry Mountain Guides (MGP).
She said the department has also cleared a winching point area about 100 square feet at Kem Salai Matahari to allow for quick evacuation or logistics if a helicopter is needed.
She said this is to make it easier for the rescue team to move, as the current winching points at Bukit Batu Putih and Bukit Berapit 2 are too far from the primary search area.
Sayani said the steep, mountainous terrain poses a challenge for the rescue team, with travel to some search locations taking 7 to 8 hours.
Jaslinda was reported to have started the Trans Spencer Chapman climb at 2 am on May 23 with 13 other hikers and two mountain guides, via the Pos Gedung-Gunung Bah Gading-Gunung Batu Putih-Kuala Woh route.
She and another hiker, Mohd Hanafi Neikmad, 41, faced health issues and stopped, but Jaslinda was reported to have continued alone to the mountain summit and was last seen by a mountain guide at 7.30 am on May 24. — Bernama

Title: After hot day, MetMalaysia forecasts stormy night across central and southern peninsula, Sabah and Sarawak
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR, May 28 — Thunderstorms are forecast in parts of Perak, Terengganu, Pahang, Negeri Sembilan, Johor, Sarawak and Sabah until 9pm, the Malaysian Meteorological Department (MetMalaysia) said this afternoon.
Affected areas in Perak include Kerian, Larut, Matang dan Selama, Hulu Perak, Kuala Kangsar, Kinta and Kampar.
In Terengganu, the warning covers Kuala Nerus, Hulu Terengganu, Kuala Terengganu, Marang, Dungun and Kemaman.
Affected areas in Pahang are Jerantut, Temerloh, Maran, Kuantan, Bera and Rompin, while in Negeri Sembilan it involves Jelebu, Kuala Pilah, Jempol and Tampin.
The warning also covers Segamat and Mersing in Johor.
In Sarawak, the affected areas are Kapit, Bukit Mabong, Tatau, Sebauh and Limbang, while in Sabah it involves Kinabatangan in Sandakan.
MetMalaysia said the warning is issued when there are signs of thunderstorms with rainfall intensity exceeding 20mm per hour that are occurring or expected to occur for more than an hour.
